articles.title: Descendants of Zimbabwe resistance heroes urge UK to locate looted skulls
articles.description: Relatives call on institutions to help them find remains of ancestors who led fight against British colonisers in 1890s

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Opposition Withdraws From Constitutional Hearings After Violence
more.description: [263Chat] Opposition leaders in Zimbabwe say they are disengaging from the ongoing public hearings on Constitutional Amendment No. 3, describing the process as "choreographed" and not genuinely consultative.

more.description: (MENAFN - IANS) Harare, April 2 (IANS) Zimbabwe Cricket has announced that they will host Australia for a three-match ODI series in September, marking Australia's first ODI visit to the country in ...

more.title: Three Harare City Council Employees Arrested Over US$500k Fraud Scheme
more.description: Three Harare City Council Employees Arrested Over US$500k Fraud Scheme Three Harare City Council employees have been arrested and charged with fraud after allegedly orchestrating a scheme that cost the local authority more than US$500 000. more.title: Govt Shifts To Performance-Based Remuneration For Civil Servants
more.description: Source: Govt Shifts To Performance-Based Remuneration For Civil Servants ⋆ Pindula News The government has commenced a major overhaul of public sector salaries, starting on 1 April, under a new remuneration framework designed to align pay with employees’ roles, responsibilities, qualifications, and experience. more.title: Four arrested as Zimbabwe moves to repatriate citizens trafficked to fight in Russia-Ukraine war
more.description: The Zimbabwean government is working to repatriate four Zimbabweans who were fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, while documentation challenges have stalled efforts to bring back others, government spokesperson Nick Mangwana has said.

more.title: Chinese Lithium Miners Deny Smuggling, Blame Unregistered Operators
more.description: Chinese lithium mining companies operating in Zimbabwe have broken their silence on the smuggling scandal that led to the government’s shock ban on raw mineral exports, insisting they are not the ones bleeding the country dry.

more.title: Jehovah’s Witnesses lose US$24k to procurement head
more.description: Source: Jehovah’s Witnesses lose US$24k to procurement head –Newsday Zimbabwe Jehovah’s Witnesses in Zimbabwe lost more than US$24 000 after a senior employee in its purchasing department allegedly diverted proceeds from auction sales of church property. more.description: In a move that has sent shockwaves of nostalgia and excitement through the broadcasting industry, Tichafa Matambanadzo, the man the continent knows and loves as Tich Mataz, has officially joined Channel Africa.
